title: Higher-order BIF trampoline — wire closure-calling stubs
purpose: Six registered BIF stubs return identity/badarg because they accept BEAM closures as arguments but the BIF calling convention cannot re-enter the interpreter to invoke them. The trampoline mechanism (TrampolineRequest + NativeContinuation + handle_native_continuation) already exists and is production-tested for maps:fold/3, maps:filter/2, lists:map/2, lists:filter/2. These stubs need to be wired into the same pattern. maps:map/2 applies a function to every value in a map. gleam@option:map/2 applies a function to the Some payload. gleam@result:map_error/2 and gleam@result:then/2 transform Result tuples. gleam@otp@intensity_tracker:new/2 and add_event/1 are fixed-shape stubs that need real tuple construction but do not need closures. The JIT path (compiled code -> jit_call_interpreted -> interpreter -> BIF -> trampoline -> interpreter -> continuation) must work without modification because the trampoline is handled inside the interpreter run_loop, which jit_call_interpreted already calls.
task: Replace the 6 identity/badarg stubs with real implementations using the existing trampoline infrastructure. Add NativeContinuation variants for maps:map and gleam result/option higher-order functions. Verify the JIT -> trampoline path works end-to-end with a test that compiles a function calling maps:map/2 with a closure argument.

requirements.title: maps:map/2 trampoline implementation
requirements.spec: WHEN maps:map/2 is called with a function and a map, THE SYSTEM SHALL extract map entries, call context.set_continuation_trampoline() with the function, first key-value pair, and a MapsHofState::Map continuation, and return Term::NIL to signal pending trampoline. WHEN the continuation resumes with the closure result, THE SYSTEM SHALL store the transformed value and advance to the next entry. WHEN all entries are processed, THE SYSTEM SHALL construct a new map from the transformed entries. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT modify the original map.
acceptance: maps:map/2 with identity function returns map with same entries
maps:map/2 with fn(K, V) -> V+1 on #{a => 1, b => 2} returns #{a => 2, b => 3}
maps:map/2 on empty map returns empty map
MapsHofState::Map variant exists in NativeContinuation::Maps
resume_maps_continuation handles Map state progression
Original map is not mutated

requirements.title: gleam@option:map/2 trampoline implementation
requirements.spec: WHEN gleam@option:map/2 is called with an option tuple and a function, THE SYSTEM SHALL inspect the option tag. IF the option is {some, Value}, THE SYSTEM SHALL call context.set_continuation_trampoline() with the function, [Value] args, and a GleamOption::Map continuation. WHEN the continuation resumes, THE SYSTEM SHALL wrap the result in {some, Result}. IF the option is none, THE SYSTEM SHALL return none without invoking the function.
acceptance: option:map({some, 1}, fn(x) -> x+1) returns {some, 2}
option:map(none, fn(x) -> x+1) returns none without calling the function
NativeContinuation has a GleamOption variant with Map state
handle_native_continuation dispatches GleamOption correctly

requirements.title: gleam@result:map_error/2 and then/2 trampoline implementations
requirements.spec: WHEN gleam@result:map_error/2 is called with {error, Reason} and a function, THE SYSTEM SHALL trampoline to invoke the function with [Reason] and wrap the result in {error, Result}. IF called with {ok, Value}, THE SYSTEM SHALL return {ok, Value} without invoking the function. WHEN gleam@result:then/2 is called with {ok, Value} and a function, THE SYSTEM SHALL trampoline to invoke the function with [Value] and return the closure result directly. IF called with {error, Reason}, THE SYSTEM SHALL return {error, Reason} without invoking the function. THE SYSTEM SHALL use the existing GleamResultTry NativeContinuation variant or add new GleamResult variants as needed.
acceptance: result:map_error({error, oops}, fn(r) -> {mapped, r}) returns {error, {mapped, oops}}
result:map_error({ok, 42}, fn(_) -> crash) returns {ok, 42} without calling function
result:then({ok, 1}, fn(x) -> {ok, x+1}) returns {ok, 2}
result:then({error, e}, fn(_) -> crash) returns {error, e} without calling function
Continuation handles GleamResult state correctly

requirements.title: gleam@otp@intensity_tracker real implementations
requirements.spec: WHEN intensity_tracker:new/2 is called with limit and period, THE SYSTEM SHALL construct a tracker tuple {0, limit, period, []} where element 0 is the event count, element 3 is the event timestamp list. WHEN intensity_tracker:add_event/1 is called with a tracker, THE SYSTEM SHALL increment the count and return {ok, updated_tracker} if count < limit, or {error, updated_tracker} if count >= limit. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require trampoline support — these functions do not accept closure arguments.
acceptance: intensity_tracker:new(5, 1000) returns {0, 5, 1000, []}
intensity_tracker:add_event(tracker) with count < limit returns {ok, updated}
intensity_tracker:add_event(tracker) with count >= limit returns {error, updated}
No trampoline request is set — these are pure data transforms

requirements.title: JIT-to-trampoline end-to-end verification
requirements.spec: WHEN JIT-compiled code calls an external function that is a BIF with trampoline support (e.g. maps:map/2), THE SYSTEM SHALL correctly execute the trampoline chain through jit_call_interpreted -> run_with_registry -> BIF -> trampoline -> interpreter -> continuation -> return. THE SYSTEM SHALL return the correct result to the compiled caller. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require any changes to the JIT dispatch or runtime helpers — the existing jit_call_interpreted path through run_with_registry already handles trampolines.
acceptance: Differential test: maps:map(fun(K,V) -> V+1 end, #{a => 1}) through interpreter and JIT produce identical results
Integration test: compiled function calling maps:map/2 returns correct transformed map
No modifications to jit/runtime.rs or jit/compiler.rs needed

requirements.title: Trampoline SHALL NOT constraints
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT add new NativeContinuation variants without corresponding resume handlers in handle_native_continuation. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return Term::NIL from a BIF without first setting a trampoline request — Term::NIL is the sentinel that triggers trampoline dispatch. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allocate heap terms inside continuation resume handlers without ensuring the process heap has space (use context.alloc_tuple or equivalent). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT modify ProcessContext.trampoline field directly — use set_continuation_trampoline() or set_trampoline().
acceptance: Every NativeContinuation variant has a match arm in handle_native_continuation
No BIF returns Term::NIL without prior set_continuation_trampoline call
All tuple allocations in resume handlers use context.alloc_tuple or process heap alloc with GC check
All trampoline field access goes through accessor methods

in_scope: Wire 4 closure-calling stubs into existing trampoline mechanism
Fix 2 intensity_tracker stubs with real tuple construction
Add NativeContinuation variants for option/result higher-order patterns
Verify JIT -> trampoline path with differential test
out_of_scope: Bytecode stdlib approach (compile Gleam stdlib to .beam and embed)
JIT-native closure invocation (compiled code calling closures directly without interpreter)
Cross-module BIF calls from embedded .beam modules

verification: cargo test --package beamr --lib && cargo test --package beamr --test differential
